content_markdown: Which of the following graphs best shows how photon energy $E$ varies with the wavelength $\lambda$ of the light?

diagram_metadata.description: Four graphs showing the relationship between photon energy E on the y-axis and wavelength lambda on the x-axis. Graph A is a linear increase, Graph B is an exponential/upward curve, Graph C is a linear decrease, and Graph D is a hyperbolic decrease.

vibe_explanation: The energy of a photon is given by the equation $E = \frac{hc}{\lambda}$. This represents an inverse relationship where $E \propto \frac{1}{\lambda}$. Mathematically, this produces a rectangular hyperbola graph where the energy decreases as the wavelength increases, approaching the axes asymptotically. Graph D correctly illustrates this non-linear inverse relationship.
